reviews.text: after I went back to New Zealand, I checked my bank balance and found the hotel didnot refund my deposit and there was an unreasonable withdrawal in this resort. I sent email but no answer at all. This is my last night in Fiji. It is very bad ending.

reviews.title: Terrible! I have stayed here in all my visit to Fiji since 2003, but I won't come back here , ever!
reviews.positives: I booked for 2 adults and 2 children all online through booking.com and even included the children's ages. The package included breakfast for everyone. When checking in the gentleman said that one of my children doesn't have breakfast and had to pay extra. When I showed my booking, he said it doesn't matter I still have to pay, as its there policy. When I asked for the policy he declined to show. Therefore I didn't get any breakfast as I refused to pay for one extra. This was suppose to be a family holiday. The same gentleman checked us into a room facing directly the restaurant, when I went back and showed him that my booking was for beachfront, he mentioned with a smile that the room is the best one for beachfront. I advised him that I regularly come to Fiji and have stayed here many times and this is not the beachfront I had booked. I asked for the manager and this is when he changed my room to the correct one as he didn't want the manager involved. I really don't understand why he would check us into a room that I hadn't booked. Further to this I have photos of the property and the condition its in, which is very poor. The only great thing was the cleaning ladies, they did a great job, were humble and worked with a smile.
reviews.negatives: Checked us into the wrong room, extra charges when clearly I had paid for specific things such as breakfast.

reviews.title: Above average

reviews.negatives: No Beach, No TV Stations, Far away from everything, Few Activities

reviews.title: Terrible Service.
reviews.text: Terrible Service and you're asked to spend 100s of dollars for every little thing after paying for your room

reviews.title: If I go to Fiji again anytime in future, I will avoid Double Tree
reviews.positives: Overall the facility has good views, pool
reviews.negatives:
Extremely bad customer service. Each person says/promise differently and you have to follow up several times to get even small things done. They charge 3% on top of the rates advertised for paying by card, which is a surprise and I have to bare that additional 3% on my invoice as was not aware of this and didn't have enough cash while check-in. I understand that some business charge additional for using debit/credit card, but all other business usually charge 2%  (2.5% max). It should be listed clearly to make people aware for this charges.
Food options in the restaurant is too bad, we tried so called "Indian" buffet, total rip-off, extremely costly, and worst food I tasted. It was no way "Indian" at all and they don't have enough quantity at the counter while refill is too slow keeping most of the counters empty quite frequently.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.positives: Waterfront
reviews.negatives: Shower corner was poorly patched with caulking- new layers over old layers.  Misleading advertised photo showed a corner beachfront unit - we were not given the corner unit.

reviews.title: Very Very Very Poor Experience
reviews.text:
Very very poor experience and it’s a toss the board, food, reception, house keeping 

When we got to the hotel side of the river, there was no porter, we had to walk with our luggage to the reception in the rain. 
Next they gave us the keys to the room at the reception and again there was no porter to take our luggage to the room, which we had to carry across the resort to the rooms on different levels. 
Once we got to the room, the room was still not ready. Although the house keeping staff were courteous.
Then we called room service 4 times and there was no answer in the end the reception told us they will call us and it took room service another 30 mins before they even called us. 
At the restaurant we were told the buffer would be good hover disappoint again the food was at best below average in case of lamb curry was all bones and no meat at all. 
Again the breakfast buffet was very average, hey but when you get below average, average is good isn’t. 
At the time of checkout again we had to wait for 45 mins before a porter could come, not to mention we got calls from housekeeping as to when we will be leaving the room, well if you send the porter on time we would have left earlier.
So a really pathetic experience 
If you really care you will do something about it.
reviews.room_type: Double Queen Guest Room Beachfront W/ Balcony

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.positives: Beautiful set up and area. Room was accommodating and met our needs
reviews.negatives: The service for a Hilton property was subpar. We didn't have anyone refresh our room (clean towels and pick up room service tray). We ordered room service and only received part of the order... charged for what we got, same at beach bar 2nd day.

reviews.title: Customer service is nil
reviews.text: Bad experience from porter to housekeeping and service staff.

reviews.title: Very good

reviews.negatives: The quality of the fruit at the breakfast buffet.

reviews.title: Service no good, old amenities
reviews.text:
Front desk staff was not friendly, hard to believe they work in the front desk. 
Some staff was great tho, especially Sushmita from F&B. 
The rooms are old, we had bathroom sink leak, ac leak, and the fly screen door to the balcony was off the rail. 
Ants on the desk, tried to clean and keep all food away, still there the whole time when we were there. 
Very limited food options, only three restaurants you can go, two of them are not opened for lunch. 
From placing the order to getting the food on the table, it took forever. And whenever we asked for something, either never came or came in very late. 
Towels were not clean, you can easily find a ball of hair or brown/green stain on the fresh towels, again, asked for replacement and never came. 
We paid more than  *** a night, which wasn’t cheap, and everything else was additional cost. 
If you have too much money, don’t care about how unorganized people are and like bad services, you should come, otherwise, not recommend Double tree Hilton and Fiji

reviews.title: Poor security
reviews.text:
Nice Hotel, but Poor Security

The hotel is nice, but my experience was ruined by a serious security issue. Someone broke into my room while I was sleeping, and there was a CCTV blind spot that left the incident unrecorded. The hotel took no responsibility or offered any resolution. I won’t be staying here again due to these security concerns.

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.text: This guest did not leave comments about this hotel.
reviews.positives: All of it - the warm and helpful staff, the beautuful hut accommodations, the AMAZING food. I just can't say enough. It was a wonderful place to visit!
reviews.negatives: The access into the pools could be better for those who are unsure on their feet - like handrails and smaller steps into the main pool area in more than one place.

reviews.title: Badly kept grounds and bed bugs
reviews.text:
I definitely got bitten by bed bugs at this place. And I emailed after my stay to request the hotel inspect the room they also didn't reply.  

I also felt that for a hilton resort, the grounds were not in very good condition and not well maintained.  Some of the seating in the gardens were broken and look like no one could be bothered to repair them.  The resort was also a little out of the way so you need to cab to get anywhere nice. Also no beach to swim in, in the resort - mainly surrounded by muddy swamp water.  Only have a hotel pool.  In terms of service, I also felt a number of the staff just didn't care.  Things got done, but not very promptly or with great professionalism.
reviews.room_type: Double Queen Guest Room Beachfront W/ Balcony

reviews.title: I would not stay here again if my life depended on it. It's a beach resort without a beach.
reviews.positives: Honestly, The only thing this resort was good for was its proximity to several Day trip's to Koroyanitu Forest and Falls, and a few others off site excursions.
reviews.negatives:
1) Staff stole money from my wife and I and didn't take responsibility  for it.
2) There is no real nice beach on the property. the entire front of the photos is a like, there is a wall of oversized rocks to keep the rest of the island from eroding away, and so while it looks great, when you get there, There isn't any beach to sit on. and if you walk down towards the activity area which isn't long, the beach is not maintained and isn't pretty, but also not safe, there are broken glass bottles everywhere and all sorts of drift material just lying on the beach and the horses they use for the tours are just dedicating on the beach and they do not pick it up. 
3) Food was not good AT ALL.  we had to be very strategic with food since most of it was not appetizing.
4) Facilities are Small. It has a great entrance via the little boat, but the pool facilities, the bar, the restaurants, - there was nothing to do and the facilities were falling apart, either not looked after, not maintained holes in the floor boards, shingles falling off the Villa's , ... it was borderline in disrepair. there was even a giant hole being covered up by a picture .. as if that would fix the problem.
5) Staff were not attentive and I'm not sure if it's just cultural, but they hardly ever provided water at meals, they would start one task and forget about it and it would be an hour at a meal before they might remember, it seemed as if small tasks were impossible to them like asking to fix some items in our rooms.
6) We felt like an inconvenience to the staff at all times. 
7) Drinks were terrible. it was like they were never properly trained to make any drinks, -- So I drank the Beer instead.

We made the best of it, had a laugh, but we had to leave site everyday for edible / quality food or drinks, things to do, and just to get a break from the place. Do not be deceived by the photos. 

The rooms were Adequate, at best.

reviews.negatives: The only way to get there is by boat but it was quick and complimentary

reviews.title: I am extremely disappointed with my stay at this hotel.
reviews.text:
The room was in an unacceptable condition, full of bugs, including large cockroaches and what appeared to be bed bugs. When I reported the issue to reception, the staff showed little concern and simply responded with “really? sorry” without taking any meaningful action. The second time I raised the issue, I was asked, “did the cleaner not come to your room?” which completely missed the point, as the problem was clearly not resolved. As a result, I have been bitten all over both of my legs during my stay.

I had booked a “special” birthday dinner package for around $300. I was told by three different staff members at the time of booking that this would include a bottle of wine or champagne. However, I only received a single glass of a drink. The food itself was no different from what you would get at their regular restaurant, which costs around $80+. This makes the $300 price completely unjustified.

Overall, this experience was extremely disappointing. Poor hygiene and misleading information about paid services made the stay unacceptable. I would strongly advise others not to book the “special” dinner package, as it is not worth the price. I will definitely not be booking this hotel again if I return to Fiji.

reviews.title: Very disappointing and overpriced
reviews.positives: Very friendly staff, quite nice grounds
reviews.negatives: My husband got very sick there from the food (diagnosed when we got home - probably unclean water and poorly cooked chicken) and they tried to use my credit card illegally but ANZ bank fortunately stopped the transaction and cancelled my card. In addition, the whole complex is very dated (45 years old) and needs refurbishing as does all the furniture. Stayed in a bure which was so dark with no overhead lighting at all. Not what you expect from a "Hilton". The breakfast buffet and the Christmas buffet were very ordinary. Plenty of variety but the quality is not there and not up to expected "western" standard. There is also a major problem with birds at the restaurant - they are a pest eating off unattended plates and pooping everywhere. The room servicing was disappointing as they never once replaced all the linen correctly and we had to ring to get the missing items. Also annoyed to have approx 5% public holiday added to the advertised $129 FJ price for Christmas buffet - felt deceptive not to include in the price as it was obviously on a public holiday. Would never stay there again.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: Very over priced hotel for no water activities (except pool) on site. They don't have functioning beach close to the rooms either. It is at the end of the resort. There is no activities to do on the resort except being in pool. The food very average and expensive. We stayed few other resorts payment similar amount which was much better. reviews.title: Horrible
reviews.text: Hotel is on an island with a strict no outside food policy. Reliance on Double tree’s restaurant is very high. And boy did they disappoint us. We tried very hard to have a good time but with no access to good food, it was extremely difficult. Breakfast is on till 10am, and on day one we went at 9.30am to find out most of the trays are empty. Ordered penne pasta for dinner only to be told after 45mins that they have ran out of Penne. Day 2 didn’t get any better: ordered a thali and it didn’t come with all the listed items. Completely unsatisfactory

reviews.text: The hotel delayed our check-in for 2 hours. We had to sit and wait for them because they havent done their cleaning... reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text:
本次入住南迪希尔顿逸林酒店的体验令人失望。酒店地理位置较为偏远，出行并不便利；同时，整体设施明显老旧，已无法匹配希尔顿品牌应有的标准，亟需全面翻新。

此外，员工整体缺乏工作热情，服务态度较为冷淡，缺乏主动性和应有的专业度。

尤其令人不满的是餐厅的安排问题。我们提前预订了餐厅泳池旁的桌位，但在到达用餐时却被告知没有泳池旁的位置，并被强行安排在餐厅中间的桌位。然而当时我们清楚地看到泳池边仍有多张桌位空置。在我们多次、强烈要求下，工作人员才勉强为我们更换了座位，整个过程令人非常不愉快。

Our stay at the DoubleTree by Hilton Nadi was disappointing. The hotel is located in a relatively remote area, making transportation inconvenient. In addition, the facilities are clearly outdated and no longer meet the standards expected of the Hilton brand. A comprehensive renovation is urgently needed.

The staff also appeared to lack enthusiasm and motivation, with service that felt passive and unwelcoming.

One particularly frustrating experience occurred at the restaurant. We had reserved a table by the pool in advance, but upon arrival, we were informed that no poolside tables were available and were instead placed at a table in the middle of the restaurant. This was despite the fact that several poolside tables were visibly unoccupied at the time. Only after we firmly insisted did the staff reluctantly change our seating, which made the entire dining experience unpleasant.

reviews.title: Did not enjoy

reviews.title: Very over priced resort in Fiji
reviews.text: Over priced food and drinks and very limited choice of meals on offer. Over all the rooms are a good size and the beds are comfortable but there is not that much to do and the food on offer is only just. The service is also slow and the prices for your meals and drinks is so over priced that i would not consider coming back to this place ever again. The service at the two restaurants is also not that great but the front desk staff are very nice and they are very helpful. We stayed for 5 nights but we were both  ready to leave after the  4th night.

reviews.title: Not usual Hilton standard
reviews.text: We've been to a number of different Hilton hotels in Asia and have come to expect a very high standard in terms of the building and the facilities. Unfortunately, the Double Tree was very not like this. The pool and common areas are disappointing. There are not many sun loungers and the pool is very small. On the plus side, the rooms are great and the beds are comfortable.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.negatives: Staff was not friendly. Facility aged and worn down. Location not great. Can’t go in the ocean from hotel. Overall would not recommend if visiting Fiji so somewhere else else.

reviews.title: Not Impressed! Tavu Resturant Closed! Bad service
reviews.text: This was our fourth trip. Upon our last experience we were given a complimentary upgrade to the Kind Premium Bure. But the services remain very bad as usual. We were supposed to check in at 3pm arrived at 2.45pm and were made to wait for 30min even though the booking was in advance. No apologies by the hotel. When we arrived it was raining. Staffs did not offer umbrella, our bags were wet because they carried it in the rain. During dinner There was no rain and Tavu grill and Bar was closed and we were not notified. But they where serving same menu at the buffet side. My 3yr old does not like to eat in a crowded area. So we ordered room service. And was amazed ro see the charge of $7 for room delivery. When it was the resorts fault the restaurant was closed. Stubby beers were brought to us with no openers. Waiters did not come and ask for refills. Buggy not operational during check out. You have to walk a long way to the reception than to the jetty. Potter staffs not helpful at all.
reviews.room_type: Double Queen Guest Room Beachfront W/ Balcony

reviews.title: Stay if you like waiting hours for your food. Place was clean though
reviews.negatives: They took a deposit when I checked in and was supposed to cancel the deposit on check out. Everything was paid for and they even ran my card to cancel the deposit but that amount is still being held on their end. I have contacted them multiple times and each time they deflect saying I need to contact my bank. Has nothing to do with my bank. They’re holding my money and won’t return it or won’t look into it. I’ve checked out a whole month now so the 5-7 working days to process is BS.

reviews.title: Extremely overpriced stay.
reviews.text: We checked in for 4 nights and we had to ask for some towels from the second night as we didn't get them with the room clean.  One day we had to ask 4 times but promises from room service were only a promise. I had to go to room service to collect them.  Very plain but overpriced food. Breakfast was the same every day. The only place I have experienced where they charge $7 to deliver a room service order on top of the overpriced menu. We asked to cancel the breakfast as we were leaving at 4.30 a.m. on the last day, but the hotel refused and charged us for it. The sea is near by but there is no access to the sea as no accessible sandy beach. If you don't like hotel food, then it is a struggle to find a restaurant nearby without going to Denarau or the city. However, there were many nice staff with big smiles. Would never select

reviews.title: Rooms are completely different from the pictures!
reviews.positives: The place has a great infinity pool for pictures, the pool is nice , and the rooms are large. There are oceans on both sides . The hosts were amazing, there was an authentic fijian dance when we arrived , and also we were given warm cookies that were delicious. Great hosts! There is also a market within the place that has some great souveniers.
reviews.negatives:
1. The rooms are NOT like the picture AT ALL! Nothing is similar. We took a beachfront room but had a room with a view while the pictures looked like it walks out into an ocean. The ocean that we could see was murky and dirty
2. We assumed the boat ride would be magical to an island, but no, it looks like a swamp and we also saw 2 frogs there. This made it rather dirty.
3. The place is infested with many insects. We also stayed at another hotel which was way cleaner 
4. Location is uncomfortably far from all activities, you cannot rent a car and most people won't pick you up from here for activities unless you pay them. This made it very hard for us for activities
5. Our door lock was broken and we had to get it fixed. The alacarte options for dinner has very few options, and since you're on the island, it makes it hard to go out and get anything else.
6. Breakfast isn't great - its average. The jelly and peanut butter is left open so kids were seen to mix them up with spoons (versus them having separate boxes that they'd be handed out in). We were expecting more options for breakfast, and indoor options that don't allow flies

reviews.title: Poor Service
reviews.text: We came to the hotel last night and had to wait nearly 2 hours for the dinner. It is very difficult to wait that long when you have a young kid. There is no other option here for food as the hotel is in the remote area. I really hope it does not happen with us again for next couple of days.

reviews.title: Extremely dated resort and facility. As a young family with a newborn, we didn't feel comfortable
reviews.positives: Extremely dated resort and facility. As a young family with a newborn, we didn't feel comfortable enough to stay, and ended up paying a cancelation fee and booking another resort. Maybe our own expectation was too high, but with the affiliation to Hilton, we expected more.
reviews.negatives: Extremely dated resort and facility. As a young family with a newborn, we didn't feel comfortable enough to stay, and ended up paying a cancelation fee and booking another resort. Maybe our own expectation was too high, but with the affiliation to Hilton, we expected more.

reviews.title: One star experience, five star pricing
reviews.text:
• we arrived at property 2:45pm, didn't get a room until 5pm (check-in time is 3pm)
• by the time we got to the room after 2 hour wait, the air-conditioning system in the room was broken down 
• while waiting, we tried to watch TV and there was no remote control in the room, called front desk but didn't follow up
• we didn't have the above problems resolved till 6pm, we went to the font desk and complained to the deputy manager and after dinner we were offered another room
• we moved from original room to another, upon getting into this new room, there were no towels prepared so we went back to the original room to get it
• after shower, we further realized the hair dryer was also missing, so we had to go back to the original room and got the hair dryer
• meanwhile all facilities were closed or suspended including swimming pool, horse back riding etc

And we are paying 260 USD per night for this, worst experience ever. There are other much better five star options in nearby area

reviews.title: not a pleasant experience
reviews.positives: pool
reviews.negatives: location. facilities, food

reviews.title: Staff are infuriating
reviews.text: Almost every interaction I had with staff was incredibly stressful (complete opposite you want on a relaxing holiday). Every time dealt with any staff there was problems like, overcharging for accomodation (by $500), incorrect menu prices, wrong food/drinks orders, wrong local information, bone pieces in pizza base, unattended bars etc etc,  it was EXHAUSTING. And they never fix the issues, they just smile and say “sorry, sorry” like poorly tuned robots and never offer to fix the problems let alone compensate you in any way. It just comes across as a “not my problem” and “if they don’t ask don’t tell” attitude. It is a nice location but the staff let it down tremendously.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: Everything was good. Only issue was that we had booked the Valentine's  4 course dinner. Menu had changed without any prior notice from what was advertised the day prior and no dietary requirements was asked when booking. This was an issue since we are allegic to seafood and the meal had changed from lamb and chicken to a seafood theme. Staff was able to help and provide us with substitute dishes.

reviews.title: Disspaointed
reviews.text: Our week-long stay at DoubleTree by Hilton Fiji - Sonaisali Island from June 14 to 21, 2025, was okay but fell short in several areas. The resort’s food was a major letdown. Breakfast was subpar, and dinners, like our meal at Tavu Bar and Grill—three chicken dishes and one rib for $216 FJD—were burnt and overpriced, pushing us to dine off-resort. Other guests echo the sentiment of inconsistent, costly food (***, 2025). The lack of evening entertainment, like shows, made nights dull, sending us straight to our room after dinner. The resort itself feels dated and needs upgrades, from facilities to activities, which were scarce. Constantly taking pricey taxis to the marina was frustrating—a shuttle bus would be a game-changer. Our room, booked for four, only had cutlery and glasses for two, and housekeeping removed extras daily, requiring repeated requests. On the bright side, the staff were genuinely friendly and helpful, a highlight noted in reviews (***, 2024). The oceanfront setting was stunning, but it couldn’t offset the issues. I’d rate the stay 2.5/5. With better food, more activities, and modern upgrades, this resort could shine, but it’s not there yet.

reviews.title: Reasonable
reviews.text: Friendly staff the hotel is okay. but keep in mind it's on an island not much choice of food & the quality of food aint that great either. Taxi ride can be expensive they charge $65 Fijian non negotiable each way that's close to $45 Australian each way. no near restaurant  with decent food either nearest restaurants are in Denarau Island for better options.  DoubleTree is good if you looking to relax only.

reviews.title: Overall nice and relaxing stay, BUT
reviews.text:
The location is quite isolated so if you plan to do anything other then stay put at resort, then be prepared to spend another FJ $60-$70 for Taxi (min.), or hire a car. 
Tip: Try not to hire a taxi from resort its almost double the cost. I managed to find a local taxi driver and took his phone# and called him directly everytime I needed taxi. Do confirm the fare before beginning journey though.

Large pool at the resort is the main attraction and also the bar next to pool. It was great and our 3yo had a blast everyday at the pool, and so did we.

The Double king Bure were nice and big. The bathrooms were great as well, BUT we did got visits from 1-2 small local Geckos lizards couple of nights. The balcony was nice and big overlooking ocean.

The buffet dinners were quite expensive and for vegetarians they offered disc. price but there was hardly anything other then salads. The breakfast was mostly with same items everyday. Last day we had couple of more items added.

There were some staff members who went above and beyond to help while others had no clue what's happening.

Personally, one should only stay here as a getaway for 2-3 days, anything over that will be bit boring or will have to spend more $$ for travelling to anywhere else to do things other then being at the pool. And if you are vegetarian, then good luck eating at the resort.

reviews.title: Nice pool
reviews.text: Nice pool, but location is out of the way--way out of the way.

reviews.title: Missed the mark
reviews.text: This resort didn't quite meet our expectations (for the amount of money you spend to be there). The staff were warm and welcoming, however food/drinks were quite expensive. Some of the facilities were a bit tired and the lack of an undercover bar area for when it rained was disappointing.

reviews.title: Not Great, over priced
reviews.text: Couple of issues, 1st you are quite far from everything, hotel taxi is expensive unless you call your own, only food option is there buffet each night which is very expensive or there other restaurant again quite expensive, theres no beach, if you sit by the (beach) the water is brown :( Rooms are all the same and are not noise proof as they are connected to the same wall so I could hear neighbors snore and talk (sooo annoying) rooms arnt blacked out they dont have curtains on the entrance door windows which means you wake up at first light! again super annoying. Breakfast was ok nothing super special, no concerns with hygiene clearly as no sanitizer was stationed at a dinner buffet and theres maybe 1 bottle at the bar during breakfast time that really annoyed me! I would be happy to pay a couple of hundred dollars for a room but not worth the full price at all. Gym  was pretty average also!

reviews.title: Limited Options and Location of our room was too far from the main hall area
reviews.text: The staff were great. Unfortunately, due to the location of the resort you have limited options with food. The tricky part was being allocated the towards the end of the island which was a bit of a walk to the restaurant and pool area. The buggy was good when it was available be ready to wait for a while if you call them during check out and check in time. We waited at least 45min in one instance we have an infant so walking was not always the solution. Food is a bit overpriced too. We would look to stay at Denarau area in the future.

reviews.title: not good enough
reviews.text:
1. the staff is very friendly
2. their resturant is always buffet dinner, no table service allowed in resturant, if you want to make dinner, you have to do room service for extra cost
3. room service is very late, may take 1 to 1.5 hour to send to your room
4.  they were always forgetting the cutlery when they do room service for dinner

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text:
Hotel is good and well appointed amenities. Breakfast was excellent. 
All staff very friendly except ones at front desk. Our flight was delayed and we got to hotel one day late and missed on one night stay. We mentioned to front office staff at check in and got no response or even something complementary to make us happy. 
Rooms are good and clean. Beach isn’t swimable and river water usually gets mixed up. No signs regarding beach safety.

reviews.title: Double tree isolation
reviews.text: The hotel is pretty remote accessed by boat that is 24hrs but approximately 35mins drive to city. Food is bellow standard. Staff are very welcoming friendly and will go above and beyond. Coffee machine was never working breakfast included left overs from the buffet dinner. Limited activites and no direct beach access. Would recommend to book at the port where there is plenty of facilities and restaurants.

reviews.title: Disappointing End to Fiji Holiday
reviews.text:
Stayed here on our last night in Fiji and wish we hadn’t. After coming from a weeks stay at another amazing resort, this was disappointing. Alot of the staff were unprofessional and unfriendly compared to the amazing people we met at our other resort. Resort grounds felt untidy and dirty, we only had dinner here but it was not worth the price. The pool overlooks a beach you can’t access, there is no snorkelling or water activities, only a sign saying swim at your own risk!
The room however was nice and spacious however you don’t come to Fiji to stay in your room. Save your money and book elsewhere.

reviews.title: Mixed experience

reviews.title: Loud - Very Loud - Music is loud upon arrival and never stops
reviews.text:
My wife and I love quiet, relaxed places and island life. Immediately upon exiting the taxi pop music (low-key Fijian music would be preferred if music is a must). It was was blaring throughout the entire compound, from the boat ramp that takes you across the river to the hotel, to the boat ramp on the other side to the check-in counter, and throughout the facilities. We could hear it in our rooms and never had a break from it in the restaurants or around the pool. It's exhausting. When we tried to walk further down the beach to find a quieter spot, we suddenly heard pop, pop, pop as paintball guns were being fired at targets on the beach. My god, really? 

We prefer to hear the ocean, the wind, and the birdlife when we're on vacation. 

Fortunately, we only booked one night and couldn't wait to depart. 

The staff is friendly but there's no personal connection like we found with the people at the smaller resorts on the islands not too far from the port. 

If you're looking to visit Fiji and have an authentic experience without drowning in noise or an environment that could be anywhere USA, don't stay at the Doubletree.

reviews.title: Not great
reviews.positives: Great pool, friendly staff
reviews.negatives: The location of the resort is a bit of a let down. You have to take a small boat to and from the resort to the mainland which adds a little time and inconvenience but thats ok. That being said the boat broke down for an over one day making us late for an event. The room we stayed in was on the opposite side of the island which meant we had to walk almost a half mile each time we wanted to go to the pool,eat or leave the island. This was not ideal with a small child and made it kind of miserable. We were there during a cyclone ,not the resorts fault but the roof was leaking in our bed pretty bad. The food selection and taste were not great. The water area along the ocean was dirty and not particularly pleasing to look at. There were also roaches in the room. Another let down was the fact we had no trash cans in the room and the closest outside trash can to us was a quartermile walk very frustrating. I would pass on this resort. There are much nicer within this price range. The staff were the highlight of the resort helpful and always happy.

reviews.title: One of the best rooms.
reviews.text:
Travelling with my adult daughter, I booked the twin king Ocean view.  I have to say possibly one of the biggest rooms I've ever stayed in and included everything we needed.
The resort itself is a short boat ride and located about 30 minutes from the airport.  I highly recommend having a car if you plan on venturing out.  The restaurant options were good, but food was overpriced and average, except for the breakfast which had good variety.  Staff were in most occasions friendly and attentive.  This place is great if you are going to relax and have no need to leave during your stay.  Price is a bit high for the location, as you could probably get better on the coral coast.  One final comment, the beach is non existent, so if you want the ocean, this is not the place to stay.

reviews.title: Fine, just so many better options in fiji
reviews.text: The rooms are actually really beautiful, and everything is very lovely and manicured, but unless you’re on a package deal - all the food is crazily overpriced, and there is no real beach to speak of, and a single pool so not much variety that you’d expect at this price point. No real grass space for the kids to run around.  And no great activities for the very littlies. Other similar places like Intercon and Shangri La are far outstanding when compared to this.

reviews.title: A lot of money for the type of experience
reviews.text:
So firstly, as many others have said, the staff were absolutely fantastic, the rooms were also super clean, no complaints at all.
The food was the main disappointment, you are on an island you have no options but to eat at the restaurant.
Again I feel like this isnt fault of the chefs or servers either, the overall quality of the produce and meat was very low.
The grill restaurant needs better lighting/facilities, the prices were that of a much nicer establishment and overall was poorly cooked(was definitely not thawed properly).

reviews.title: Value for money is low
reviews.text:
Pros: 
-Staff are very warm and friendly.
-Sound of ocean in front of room is so relaxing.
-Generally a nice property

Cons: 
-We had scheduled a taxi to pick us up from the airport, no one showed up. It was nice of them of give us 1 day of breakfast  free to make up for this, but still not a great start to our trip
-The lights in the room are very dim. Not good if you want to read a book. One of the light bulbs was also out.
-Housekeeping can be more thorough - we didn't have any soap on the day we arrived. Another day, the dirty towels were picked up but not replaced 
-Food is low quality, unavailable a lot of the times and super pricey.

I think they have the potential to be 5 stars, but right now a lot of things are lacking.

reviews.title: Not worth the money
reviews.text:
Pros: Great looking room, great view. Food was good. 
Cons: 1. There was a rat in our room. This is a massive hazard- especially since we have two kids in the room of which 1 crawls. We repeatedly tried calling the front desk but there was no answer. Finally when our food got delivered, we told the person delivering about the rat who immediately sent someone with Mortein. Not sure how Mortein helps with rats, but we did see rat poo near the sink (have photos if you like)  indicating that the rat is probably a common visitor. The only person who asked about the rat after that was the person from the restaurant to whom we complained; no one else was bothered. This is unacceptable in such a big brand and frankly made us uncomfortable, particularly because we paid over $350 for a night for a room.
2. The front desk staff were run off their feet- you really need more staff. Every time we called the reception, it would take us multiple tries before we got connected through. Seeing the big queues, it is understandable that they could'nt attend phones, but this means that you need to have adequate staffing.
3.  I paid to have a room with a check in time of 3PM. I arrived at the property at 4PM and my room was still not ready and had to wait. In fact, several others were also waiting for their room. This is pretty poor service, considering had I overstayed in my room, you would not have hesitated to charge me more. 
4. Extremely long waiting times for food to be delivered, especially considering that if you do not have dinner reservations, there is a long wait for that too.

All in all, I was rather disappointed with my stay at Doubletree primarily because of the rat and the slow service. Yous staff are lovely otherwise, probably rusty from having two years of downtime due to Covid, but the reason I pay a lot of money to stay is because I expect a brand like you to take care of such things. Certainly not worth the money I paid to stay.

reviews.title: Below average
reviews.text:
Stayed here 7 nights.
Location is poor. Cost $45fjd to get to port denarau and taxi drivers dont have change from a $50.
While staff were nice I feel that they lacked enthusiasm and attentiveness especially when dining. Trying to gain ones attention to order was annoying as the staff didnt look up from the task at hand. Nor were the staff engaging. Resort was under staffed
Only one restaurant is under cover.
This resort has more of a caravan park feel where people stick to themselves. There is a different vibe here to other resorts I have been to in fiji this is my 4th resort I have stayed at. And this was the worst.
Food was average.
Buffet breakfast was average.
Rooms were good nice size with good view. however we didnt have our toilet paper replenished and we ran out.
There is only one pool. Would have been better if there was an adults only pool.
Nil snorkeling. Dirty sand.
reviews.room_type: Double Queen Guest Room Beachfront W/ Balcony

reviews.title: Great value for money for a few day stay
reviews.text: Great pool that the kids don’t hours in. Nice big rooms and beds. Very friendly staff. However limited menu that feels a bit overpriced and no beach or nice spot to swim in the sea

reviews.title: Nice, but probably not again.
reviews.text:
Staff were excellent, no exceptions. Very helpful and friendly. 
Room a little dated, did leak from the roof when it rained. We had a king ocean view Bure. Unfortunately the ocean view was between 2 other bures in front of us.

The wall between the Bure next to us was paper thin and we could hear everything. 

The buffet breakfast was always luke warm to cold. The themed dinner nights were every night, we only did one, the pizza and pasta night as the pasta was inedible and there was only 2 pizzas on offer. 

We did in house dining in our room. The food was very good, hot and tasty. 

It was a nice break but we would try another resort next time.

reviews.title: Great on a budget
reviews.text: Great resort on a budget. However, a while away from anything and not much to do at the actual resort. We arrived tv and phone in the room was not working. Staff were friendly, breakfast was good. Some little things might help this resort - note for anyone staying - there are not anywhere near enough chairs / lounges.  people were securing lounges first thing in the morning and there were constantly people walking around looking for places to sit.
reviews.room_type: Double King Premium Ocean View Bure

reviews.title: Patched up roofs
reviews.text: Yes you read it correctly. I am not sure how this is a 4 star resort.  When you can see roofs patched up with plastic sheets.  Ceiling interior wood panels cracks are also visible. Ants and lizard visible in the room. If it was not for staff hospitality, I wouldn’t have given 1 star.

reviews.title: Great Island stay
reviews.text: We absolutely loved our stay at DoubleTree Fiji! The ocean view was stunning, and the walk to our cabin—surrounded by trees and nature—made us feel truly connected to the island’s beauty. From the moment we arrived, the staff made us feel pampered and special. Everyone greeted us with warm "Bula Bula!" smiles that added to the charm. Even the boat transfer to the island felt like part of the adventure. A truly unforgettable experience!

reviews.title: Nice stay

The one issue we had was the location - it's not very close to Nadi or Suva, so if you are joining a tour, I would recommend getting a transfer included.

reviews.negatives: The rooms were so dirty , lots of dust everywhere and you could see the dead insects around.

reviews.title: Disappointed
reviews.text:
It started from the car park area which was very swampy and was hard not to get the shoes soaked in muddy water.
We called for slippers which did not turn up till the 3rd call
Shower door had no rubber insulation and the water kept running out drenching the mat.
The buggy did not turn up upon request therefore we ended up walking in the rain to get to the lobby.
One of the days when we left to go out for sightseeing i called the housekeeping and requested for the room to be done requesting extra mat for the leaking shower door also hung the sign on the door for housekeeping but when we got back in the evening the room was still not done. we were so disappointed having no fresh towels as well for shower after a long day out. We had to call and wait till a housekeeper came and cleaned up at night.
One of the morning for the breakfast I took 2 hash brown one of which had a bacon clinging to it. being a vegetarian I felt disgusted and I threw it away but It put me off
Managed by Hilton we didn't expect these things to happen but it did along with few other things such as dirty beach, poor lighting on the walkway together with huge puddles of water and breakdown of transfer boat while waiting for transfer at night. 
We hope that the management looks into this and try to give better experience to the future guest.

reviews.title: A great family holiday, but the resort was tired and overpriced for what it offers
reviews.positives: Staff were great, very friendly and helpful. The rooms and facility were all very clean
reviews.negatives: Restaurants and bars close at 9.30pm which is crazy for a resort on an island. The restaurant choices are extremely limited, we ate in all three restaurants and the food was extremely average. The rooms and hotel as a whole, whilst clean, are dated and in need of an update. The air conditioning in 2 of our 3 rooms was not functioning properly, maintenance tried to fix them, but it wasnt possible, we are just glad it wasnt hotter at our time of visit.

reviews.title: a mixture of good and bad probably made better by the friendliness of all staff.
reviews.positives: After a verbal wind up the first night for poor restaurant service the word got around and the staff couln't have been nicer or more attentive
reviews.negatives: The floating pool barriers that prevented  my swimming lenghts and my wife in her eighties could not access the swim up bar.

reviews.title: Travelling with family
reviews.text: We had an Ocean view king room. Lovely location and views, large room with balcony BUT paper thin walls!! First night woken at 3.30 am with neighbours packing up to check out!! The bure is NOT a stand alone. Next morning got woken at 6 am and the same for the next 4 mornings by a crying loud child. We did mention this to staff who are obviously aware of this problem.  You can hear everything!! Staff are absolutely amazing, so very friendly and happy and very helpful. Great breakfast buffet selection, but most mornings hot food warm at most. They have a dinner theme night every night, went to the Butcher’s one, ok, but again a lot of hot food was warm, so we cancelled the Island night fearing the seafood my not be hot. Main dinner restaurant not a large menu, I presume they put more emphasis on the theme nights, so our family travelling with us opted for room service which had a better selection. Lunch menu good but wait times easily an hour. The other smaller beach front restaurant, food was  amazing. Lovely pool area with swim up bar. Nice resort to stay at and chill for a few days.

reviews.title: Very underwhelming
reviews.positives: The swim up bar
reviews.negatives: Food was not up to par with what we are used to at Fijian resorts. Parking for our rental was under a tree and so our rental was always dirty and covered in bird poop.

reviews.title: Great safe resort
reviews.text:
Wonderful location for families to spend time at. A little away from everything too, however. 
Don't bother getting cash as its a card only resort. 
Very fijian mentality about time so everything is very relaxed and slow. Not a bad thing for a tropical holiday. 
We did the horse trek which was interesting as 1 horse was a biter and the other 2 were very slow. No training or instructions so be aware when booking. 
Staff are so lovely, overall, and food was ok but not very adventurous.
reviews.room_type: Double Queen Guest Room Beachfront W/ Balcony

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: The property was well maintained and the room was spacious
reviews.negatives: The service was underwhelming for a Hilton property. There was no drinking water in the rooms and we had to buy it for 9$ (Fiji dollar) a bottle from their store which is almost 325/- which is alot. It was my friend's birthday and I had requested them to setup something in the room while we stepped out even offering to pay for it but that wasn't done and later they sent a very basic tiny cake on reminding again and left it in the room. The room wasn't cleaned even though the sign was put outside and we had to call and remind them twice. But the most disappointing was the checkout in the morning. We had called and asked them the night before if we should complete checkout at night but we were told there are people at the reception 24*7 so we need not worry. There was no one available to help us with luggage when we called for it and our checkout took almost 30 mins. When you have a flight to catch this is not the service you expect.  There is a lot that can be done to improve the service and keep the global standard for a brand especially seeing the rates for a room.

reviews.title: Peace and quiet stay away from ports
reviews.text: The hotel for our surprise was very beautiful and located away from the main port. You take a small boat to go across to the resort. Kids Cub with Eka was a massive hit with my son. There were few challenges regarding the Tavua grill restaurant, I won’t recommend going there, the service is really slow and the food is below average with very high price. It’s better to experience the buffet. Would definitely love to go back.

reviews.title: Lovely Getaway
reviews.text: The facilities are nice, the rooms are beautiful (however the walls are a little thin so you can hear the neighbours very clearly). The food is a bit underwhelming and limited vegetarian options and comes out on "Fiji Time" as do the drinks unless you order directly from the bar. The buffets are great but a buffet breakfast everyday and then buffet dinner gets a bit much. Hope for nice weather otherwise the activities are a bit limited

reviews.title: Pros and Cons
reviews.text: It was our first time to Fiji and will certainly return. The staff were great and in particular Jonie should be given a promotion and raise, he was great. We found the buffet experience too expensive for what we could actually eat and we thought  the in room menu was better than the ala carte restaurant. Ensure to download your own entertainment and bring books.  Wi-fi is not free unless you are enrolled in Hilton Honors.  The boat transport to the hotel is quite efficient and runs 24 hours. If you use the resort based flat fare taxi you will pay double over calling your own metered taxi. Roughly $30 to Denarau Island versus $60.  The pool is great and kept tidy. This resort is typically less expensive than those on Denarau Island and worth the stay if you come prepared.

reviews.title: Fancy and overpriced

reviews.title: Lovely hotel generally, but getting dated
reviews.text:
Let’s start with the good things to report. 

POSITIVES:  We loved the pool, the area around it, good cocktails at the pool bar, comfortable pool loungers. Food was great too!  Lovely gardens and setting. 

I think in its heyday it would have been magnificent. It is showing many signs of wear & tear that needs addressing - eg. peeling paintwork, tiles missing from the pool. 

We paid quite a lot per night for an ocean glimpses, ‘Ocean View Bure (cabin), but the cheaper ‘Ocean Front Queen guest room’ was better value for money with a great ocean view. My parents had booked this one. The Bure was roomy but that was the only thing going for it. The mattress was comfortable, but bounced with EVERY little movement that my partner made, and I honestly was jolted awake every time he moved. In the 2 nights we stayed I had very little sleep. I think because it was a spring mattress, but not sure. This hotel chooses to use a fitted sheet & a doona only  so I was either boiling hot with it on, or freezing with it off. The air-conditioning with an additional 2 ceiling fans just didn’t get the room cold enough to comfortably use a doona. A flat sheet would have solved this problem. Also, the toilet seat moved A LOT when used, like right off the toilet, and the complimentary body lotion pump was broken. The wardrobes were not deep enough to even fit a std cabin in when opened, so we couldn’t close the wardrobe door - kinda annoying and a bad design. 

All in all, I expected a bit better of a Double Tree hotel.

reviews.title: Good Accommodations
reviews.text: Accommodations were great. Just wished there were more TV Channels instead of 8 or less. Only a few channels had sound.  Hilton should look into this.

breakfast is ok but always about 20-30% some dish is runout or the pots
are empty, 
you can’t get a taxi, if the hotel call the taxi, the taxi drive will charge 3-4 times more then meter rate, so you have no choice, you have pay 3-4  times more for the food, that you really don’t like, or you have to pay taxi driver 110-120 dollars (AUD) to go eat out side, so basically you are trapped,  the building is super old, even the toilet and shower doesn’t have exhaust  fan , so except some smells 😅 or leave the windows open, then deal with ants and small insects, 
The stuff are good

reviews.title: Agoda is the responsible for our thumbs down!
reviews.text:
Agoda did not book a room with breakfast included, even though we paid for breakfast.
This is a common mistake to make money from customers. It wasn’t the hotels fault. 
Even though I paid for a room with breakfast you submitted a request for a room without it, so you pay less to the hotel. Shame.

reviews.title: Beautiful island getaway
reviews.text: We stayed as a couple for 9 days. Location- good 15 min from the town, it is a 3min ferry across available 24/7. The staff were exceptional, very interactive and helpful. This made our stay special. The pool was big enough with volley ball. Negatives- no real beach. We had a leaky roof  after heavy rain, they did upgrade us to beach front which was lovely.  Food - Asian night was not the best food, Saturday night was burger night! The rest of the nights were good and good value. Not much to watch on TV when it rains. It did rain 3 days. We did rent a car as taxis were so expensive. It was a lovely stay  for our first visit and think lovely for families as lots of space on the island and activities like table tennis, volley ball and horse riding. For couples think Crowne Plaza would have been a better choice.

reviews.title: Great view from bure
reviews.text:
Over all, Had a very relaxing stay. 

Pros: quiet and far from city.   Clean bure with comfortable bed and awesome view.  Friendly staff. We had a late flight and was able to check out late. 

Cons:  not much activities on site except pool. You’ll have to book tours or take taxi out of resort area. Food selection was adequate with only 2 restaurants. The beach is murky due it’s in a bay with black sand.  You may still swim but at your risk. 

Suggestion, Kayaking or paddle boarding would be perfect for this area as the waters are calmer.

reviews.text: Very relaxing, staff fantastic but Ocean View has numerous mosquitoes to kill. Upgraded to Premiere Ocean Front and lounge had flee’s until it was sprayed underneath and controlled. Staff were very helpful and friendly which helped enormously.

reviews.title: Nice Stay
reviews.text:
The hotel take about 40 mins from the airport, there’s no ‘Uber’ or anything similar in Fiji so you either get a taxi or rent a car which I highly recommended if you want to explore the island. 
Beautiful room, private complex and it’s nice for relax. The facilities was not bad either, got nice pool, tennis court, gym and a little open play room.
Option of foods are quite good with different theme buffet every  night. 
The only thing is the sea water right on the hotel not good for swimming so that was a pity as we were looking forward to it.
The view of Sunset was amazing from the hotel.
So overall it was ok for us.

reviews.title: Great hotel for a relaxing family holiday
reviews.text: This was our first time in Fiji, Doubletree is a lovely hotel and cheaper alternative to its cousin in Denarau Island. Location is a drive from the port but not too costly ($40-$50fjd). It has one pool that sits in front of the ocean, was never too busy but would be crowded if the hotel was full. Food options were limited if not opting for the costly evening themed buffets and expect to always wait over an hour if ordering from the a la cart menu. Staff were always warm and welcoming, great our daughter by name throughout the stay. Overall we had a great time, I’d suggest a couple of nights here is enough unless your doing lots of day trips to the islands that I highly recommend.
reviews.room_type: Double King Premium Ocean View Bure

reviews.title: Pleased with stay
reviews.text: Double Tree was a good stay. My kids were happy with stay too.  The only thing i have to comment about is upon walking into the Bure bathroom area for the first time, we smelled a unpleasant odor emanating from the shower & sink drainage. On another note, some of the channels on the room TV  didn’t have any sound. When maintenance came by, they said they had no control over the sound issue, but said that the main station had control - i find that unsatisfactory.

It suited our needs well as a young family needing to relax, and it was a quiet place to do this. However, for those expecting a pristine white sand beach, you will need to look elsewhere.

reviews.text:
Had a wonderful couple of days at the resort.

We loved staying in the bure, which was close to the water and convenient to see the sunset. Note that, although on an island, there's no beach on the island, but a rocky embankment. It's also a good distance away from Nadi city and Denarau, so factor that in.

The boat that connects the resort to the other side for access to taxis, tours etc. is almost constantly plying between the two sides and is good fun.

reviews.title: Excellence in customer service but quiet isolated location
reviews.text:
The complex is stunning, staff are friendly, professional & well trained.
Food is Ok.
Location can be an issue if you are travelling with the family and it might impact on your budget significantly. No easy access to grocery shops, restaurants or central locations where you can book excursions at a reasonable price.
Taxis know this and ask for AU$ 21 one way to take you to the nearest grocery shop only 2,5 Km away.
Bottom line when it comes to food & excursions (without a bit of research before arrival), you are stuck to source it within the complex at much more expensive cost that you can find it elsewhere

reviews.text:
Rooms are exceptionally cozy and comfortable, and very spacious. We had a beachfront bure with a beautiful view.  
The facilities are very modern. We especially enjoyed the beachfront massage and facial in the pagode - it was fantastic. The staff were also extremely nice and welcoming - especially the restaurant staff for the evening beachfront dinners and the porters. We also enjoyed the dance shows and traditional dinners. 
The only thing issue we had was with the beach itself. The sand seems artificial and the waters are very murky. Not pleasant to swim in. Also, the pool bar was playing very loud music all the time. And the service in the shops was a bit slow. But otherwise, we enjoyed our stay.

reviews.title: Lovely family holiday
reviews.text:
Our stay at DoubleTree was exactly what we were after. It was relaxing, set in a lovely location, and had  wonderful staff. 
The room, restaurant, and pool were clean and tidy.
Breakfast was solid and filled us up enough where we skipped lunch all week.
We mostly ate dinner at the buffet which was easy with the kids, and tasty. The staff were attentive without being overbearing. They remembered our drinks orders from night to night.
The quality of the buffet food was good, and as you would expect from a buffet. The quality of the food at à la carte dining was better, as you would also expect.
The location is lovely, though a little secluded. Perfect for a quiet getaway, but a little harder to use as a base for exploring Fiji.

reviews.title: A Lovely Resort Experience in Fiji
reviews.text:
I stayed here as a solo traveller and overall had such a relaxing and memorable experience. The resort itself is beautiful, peaceful and perfect if you’re looking to unwind and slow down for a few days. The staff were warm and welcoming, and I always felt comfortable around the resort.

The buffet breakfast each morning was lovely, and I enjoyed spending my days by the water, having massages and simply relaxing. The resort also made it easy to organise activities and transport when needed.

The rooms were comfortable and the atmosphere around the resort was calm and tropical exactly what I needed for a quiet getaway.

I’d especially recommend this resort for anyone wanting a more laid-back Fiji experience away from the busier tourist areas.

Thank you for a beautiful stay 🌴

reviews.title: Staff, room, activities were excellent
reviews.text:
I had a wonderful stay at DoubleTree Resort by Hilton Fiji Sonaisali Island. From the moment we arrived, the staff made us feel incredibly welcome and well taken care of.

A special thank you to Eka, who truly made the experience unforgettable. Her energy, friendliness and the activities she organized (morning walks, t-shirt painting, tree planting) were amazing and added so much fun to our stay. She went above and beyond to make sure we were engaged and enjoying ourselves.

The room was very clean, comfortable and offered a beautiful view, perfect for relaxing after a day of activities. Small request, if there was sunlounges on the decks of the rooms, it would be even more amazing. 

The food was also good.

Overall, it was a fantastic experience and I would definitely recommend this resort to anyone looking for a relaxing and enjoyable getaway.

Thank you for the great stay! We'll be back.

reviews.title: A great family getaway
reviews.text:
When we arrived on the first day, everything was great. The room and facilities met our expectations, and check-in was smooth. Dinner on the first night was okay, and breakfast was excellent with a good variety and quality.

The issue started on Christmas Day. We were not informed at check-in that dinner required a reservation, nor were we made aware that there would be an additional charge of FJD ** per person, despite buffet dinner being listed as inclusive in our booking.

When my family and I went for dinner, we were asked if we had a reservation. This was confusing, as we had not been told that reservations were required, and our room number was not listed. Given that dinner was included in our booking, clearer communication at check-in would have avoided this situation.

Regardless of these issues, the rest of our stay went well overall. However, we did notice a difference in how local and international guests were treated, particularly at the restaurants, where international guests appeared to receive more attentive service compared to locals.

That said, I would like to commend Ms. Tara from the front desk and Ms. Samantha from the breakfast shift. Both were extremely helpful, professional, and made a positive difference to our stay.

reviews.title: Highly Recommended - family Friendly
reviews.text:
We had an amazing stay at the DoubleTree Hotel Resort. The whole place is huge and beautifully set out. When you arrive at the entrance, they transfer you by boat to the main hotel reception — such a unique and fun experience!

Our room was very comfortable, and they also have beautiful private villas for extra privacy. Even when our door lock battery went low, one of the engineers came quickly, fixed it with no fuss, and even refreshed our room again. Everyone here is so hospitable, warm, and genuinely happy to help.

One of the best parts is that when you’re relaxing near the shore, you can simply order food via QR code, and they deliver it right to you. Super convenient!

Overall, we had a wonderful time — highly recommended for anyone visiting Fiji!

reviews.title: Welcome Home
reviews.text:
Epic!! We did a 5 night stay, the sunset river cruise and paintball target shooting. 

No matter where you are in the resort, or who you come across - the team at Double Tree deliver an outstanding service and experience. 

The manager and front of house crew; SP, Margaret, Samantha were outstanding, kind and generous.  The bar and restaurant staff greeted us by name - nothing is too much trouble. 

My girls (8/10) have NEVER wanted to join Kids Club activities but after a chance encounter with Eka, they asked to sign up…. Every day!! Eka and the kids club team are kind, loving and welcoming to children - such a neat lady. 

Can’t wait to come back and stay at the Double Tree! The food is lovely -  generous buffet breakfast. Meals at various restaurants were really good. Only feedback would be a few more choice for kids meals….. but with that said, my two are a bit picky. 

Loved the river cruise, you can walk along the beach and enjoy a few happy hour cocktails throughout the day. The grounds are kept impeccable and there are neat cushioned bed/swings hanging from trees to kick back and read / relax around the resort. 

Short 20-30cm in drive from the airport.

Can’t fault it, and definitely looking forward to staying again.

reviews.title: Review for DoubleTree by Hilton Fiji ⭐️⭐️⭐️ (3/5)
reviews.positives:
What I Liked:
	•	The resort has a generally nice vibe and is a good place to relax.
	•	Staff were polite and courteous during our stay.
	•	Comfortable environment if you’re just looking to unwind and not too particular about the details.
reviews.negatives:
What I Didn’t Like:
	•	Not worth the price paid—InterContinental offers better value for the same cost.
	•	Room issues: peeling ceiling, loud exhaust fans in bathroom and toilet, and a broken AC screen. We couldn’t set the temperature properly and ended up sleeping in the heat.
	•	Very limited vegetarian options at breakfast and nothing available for vegetarians on the all-day dining menu.
	•	Having to go off-property for meals meant extra cost for taxis unless you have your own car or rental.
	•	Luggage storage felt insecure—left in a public area beside the front desk. No proper check-in/out process and no one watching it. We had to grab our bags ourselves after waiting to be noticed.

reviews.text: This hotel was a fantastic find! Our room was clean and tidy, and the view of the ocean was breathtaking. The atmosphere is so relaxing and peaceful. A highlight of our trip was the horse riding along the beach - an absolute must-do! Highly recommend this place for a perfect getaway.

reviews.title: The longed-for South Pacific
reviews.text: In a beautifully rich natural environment, I was able to truly feel the South Pacific. Traveling between the main island and the hotel on the hotel's private boat added to the special experience. The staff were always smiling, very friendly, and kind. The pool was spacious, and the children seemed to enjoy playing there all day long. The rooms in the independently built structures were well-kept, and I enjoyed watching the sunset while feeling the sea breeze on the deck. Overall, I was very satisfied.

reviews.title: Poor buffet, not a pleasant beach, but staff makes up for all the shabby experiences. Excellent people
reviews.positives: Hydrosports offered from within the property, and the staff!! Excellent people. Always smiling. Always helpful. Will go out of the way to support. And that’s true everywhere in Fiji. The chef was so nice. He stopped by and prepared some special, spicy items for us one night. That was the highlight.
reviews.negatives: 1. I know it may not be the property’s fault, but water line broke twice, and there was no water, at night, and at noon when we had just come back from the sea and really needed a shower. 2. We took half board, bad idea. Ala Carte has so much better food. Buffet below average. 3. Our room facing the sunset was far far away. And beach had dark sand and not clean, not like you could go in and take a dip. So pool was your only choice. And if that’s the case, then just ask for a room closer to the main area. 4. AC was super inefficient. 5. Bathroom glass doors don’t seal making the whole bathroom wet.6.  It is a poor attempt at being a resort, needs a lot of work.

reviews.title: Great place to stay with family and value for money
reviews.positives:
The rooms were great and comfortable. The breakfast and dinner buffet were amazing and tasty. Lots of choices and themes. Good ambiance and location of dining area. Ample of sitting area although at peak times there is waiting sometimes. There are hidden gem areas from kids play area to relaxing spa and pools to indoor and outdoor sports activities such as carrom board, darts, volleyball, paintball target shooting, horse riding and much more. The staff was professional and caring. From the front desk staff - Serona to baggage porter - Pranil, paintball staff - Dass. It will be incomplete without the comments from my son, "Shout out to Freddy, Soko and Kav.
Thank you Freddy for your amazing hospitality and Charisma while working at the bar. Thanks for teaching us how to husk and crack open a coconut by ourselves forming a life long memory.
Thank you Soko for the absolutely wonderful customer service at times of confusion. Your ability to answer questions and naturally want to give us the experience of the restaurant was greatly appreciated.
Thank you Kav for your phenomenal bartending skills and surprisingly widespread general knowledge."
reviews.negatives: Sometimes refill of the the buffet stations took time but I understand it could be due to peak season and also indicating that probably the food was great :). The Gym lacked ventilation and fresh air, although, there was AC running and perfume auto spraying but lacked fresh air inlet or ventilation.
